title: Undergraduate foundation at University of Florida
summary: The B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from University of Florida established the engineering foundation for graduate work.

title: Graduate formation at Illinois
summary: M.S. and Ph.D. from UIUC provided rigorous training in mechanical engineering and established the research foundation for a distinguished academic career.

title: Postdoctoral research at Beckman Institute
summary: Ford Foundation Fellowship and Research Scientist position at UIUC's Beckman Institute bridged doctoral training and faculty appointment.

title: Stanford faculty appointment and laboratory founding
summary: Joining Stanford in 1998 marked the beginning of a transformative academic career, establishing the Microfluidics Laboratory and building a research program spanning decades.

title: Early career federal recognition
summary: Collegiate Inventors Award, NSF CAREER, and PECASE represented the highest federal recognition for early-career researchers, confirming national prominence.

title: Professional society fellowships
summary: Fellowships in APS, ASME, and AIMBE represented peer recognition of sustained excellence across physics, engineering, and biomedical applications.

title: Academic and editorial leadership
summary: Vice Chair of Mechanical Engineering and Editor-in-Chief of Flow demonstrate institutional and scholarly leadership extending beyond direct research contributions.

title: National academy elections
summary: Election to NAI, AAAS, and NAE represents the pinnacle of academic recognition, confirming transformative contributions to microfluidics and engineering.

title: Research productivity and scholarly impact
summary: 220+ journal papers, h-index of 91, and 1500+ annual citations demonstrate sustained research excellence and influence across three decades.

title: Mentorship legacy and academic lineage
summary: 32 PhD students and postdocs trained, with 24 now faculty at major universities, demonstrating the multiplier effect of excellent mentorship.
